items.body:
## Description
移除已废弃的 `base::__string_view` 内部别名（以双下划线前缀标识为实现细节），将所有 pub/sub-loop 使用方迁移到公开的 `base::string_view`（等价于 `std::string_view`）。双下划线前缀在 C++ 中为保留标识符，存在未定义行为风险。

```cpp
// 废弃（移除）
namespace world::base {
  using __string_view = std::string_view;  // 内部实现别名，应移除
}

// 迁移目标
namespace world::base {
  using string_view = std::string_view;    // 公开别名，保留
}

// 使用方迁移：
// 旧：world::base::__string_view channel_name
// 新：world::base::string_view  channel_name
```

## Dependencies
- 全代码库 base::__string_view 使用扫描工具
- base::string_view 公开别名定义
- CI 废弃符号检测

## Sub-tasks
- [ ] 扫描所有 base::__string_view 使用方（grep/clang-tidy）
- [ ] 逐一替换为 base::string_view（脚本辅助）
- [ ] 移除 base::__string_view 别名定义
- [ ] CI lint 规则：禁止新增 __string_view 使用
- [ ] 验证替换后所有测试通过

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 代码库中无 base::__string_view 残留（CI 检测）
- [ ] 所有使用方迁移到 base::string_view，功能不变
- [ ] CI lint 规则阻断新增双下划线 base 别名
- [ ] 全部测试通过，无功能回归
- [ ] 提交包含迁移脚本，方便外部下游迁移

[FEA] Implement individual state unit-test harness using Catch2 for base module lock-free data structures
items.body:
# [FEA] Implement Individual State Unit-Test Harness Using Catch2 for Base Module Lock-Free Data Structures

## Summary
Design and implement a comprehensive Catch2-based unit testing framework for verifying the correctness and thread-safety of lock-free data structures in the `base` module. These structures (lock-free queues, ring buffers, atomic state containers) are the foundation for zero-copy pub/sub state exchange between individuals in the world model.

## Motivation
Lock-free data structures in `base` are critical infrastructure for individual state sharing. A single race condition or ABA problem can corrupt world state silently. Without rigorous multi-threaded testing under stress, we cannot guarantee correctness of concurrent individual state access patterns.

## Requirements

### Functional Requirements
- Catch2 test suite covering all lock-free containers in `base`: `AtomicHashMap`, `BoundedQueue`, `LockFreeRingBuffer`
- Multi-threaded stress tests simulating concurrent individual state read/write patterns (N producers publishing state, M consumers subscribing)
- ABA problem detection tests for pointer-based lock-free structures
- Memory ordering verification tests (acquire/release/seq_cst boundary conditions)
- Linearizability checker for concurrent operations on shared individual state buffers

### Non-Functional Requirements
- Tests must run on both x86_64 and aarch64 (RK3568 embedded target)
- Test execution time < 60s for full suite under CI
- Support for sanitizers: TSan (thread), ASan (address), UBSan (undefined behavior)
- Integration with CTest for CI pipeline

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 100% API coverage for `BoundedQueue`, `LockFreeRingBuffer`, `AtomicHashMap`
- [ ] Stress tests with 8+ threads pass 1000 iterations without failure
- [ ] TSan clean under concurrent producer-consumer patterns
- [ ] Tests build and pass on aarch64 cross-compilation toolchain

## Design Notes
- Use Catch2 `GENERATE` for parameterized thread counts and buffer sizes
- Use Catch2 `SECTION` for setup/teardown of shared state fixtures
- Consider property-based testing via rapidcheck integration for state invariant verification

## Dependencies
- `base` module lock-free container implementations
- Catch2 v3.x (header-only)
- CMake test infrastructure

## Cyber RT Module Mapping
**base** — Lock-free data structures for concurrent individual state access

items.title: [FEA] Implement parallel lexicographical comparison for individual state ordering in base module
items.body:
# [FEA] Implement Parallel Lexicographical Comparison for Individual State Ordering

## Summary
Implement a parallel lexicographical comparison algorithm in the `base` module for efficiently ordering and comparing individual states. This enables deterministic state ordering across individuals, which is required for consensus, conflict resolution, and snapshot consistency in the world model.

## Motivation
When multiple individuals publish state updates concurrently, the world model needs a deterministic total ordering to:
1. **Conflict resolution**: When two individuals modify overlapping state, lexicographic comparison of their state vectors determines which update "wins" (similar to blockchain conflict resolution)
2. **Snapshot consistency**: Recording a consistent world snapshot requires ordering all individual states deterministically
3. **State diffing**: Computing delta-sync between two world snapshots requires comparing individual states in canonical order

A parallel implementation is necessary because individual states can be large (kilobytes of serialized protobuf) and the world may contain thousands of individuals. Sequential comparison would be a bottleneck.

## Requirements

### Functional Requirements
- `parallel_lexicographic_compare(state_a, state_b)` → `{less, equal, greater}`
- Support comparison of heterogeneous state types (via serialized byte representation)
- Support batch comparison: compare N state pairs in parallel, return ordering vector
- Early termination: stop comparing once a difference is found (critical for performance)
- Support custom comparison predicates (e.g., compare only position components, ignore metadata)

### Non-Functional Requirements
- Performance: ≥ 4x speedup over sequential `std::lexicographical_compare` for states > 1KB
- Lock-free implementation (must be usable from within `BoundedQueue` comparators)
- Works on both x86_64 (using SIMD: SSE4.2/AVX2 memcmp acceleration) and aarch64 (NEON)
- Memory: zero additional allocation for comparison (operate on state buffers in-place)

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correctness: matches `std::lexicographical_compare` for all test inputs
- [ ] Batch mode: compare 1000 state pairs in parallel
- [ ] Performance: 4x speedup on 4KB states (x86_64 benchmark)
- [ ] aarch64 NEON path works on RK3568
- [ ] Integration: used by `record` module for snapshot ordering

## Design Notes
- Partition state buffers into chunks, compare chunks in parallel, short-circuit on first mismatch
- On x86_64, use `_mm256_cmpeq_epi8` + `_mm256_movemask_epi8` for vectorized byte comparison
- On aarch64, use `vceqq_u8` + `vmaxvq_u8` NEON intrinsics
- The comparator should be compatible with `std::sort` and lock-free priority queues in `base`

## Dependencies
- `base` module (lock-free containers, SIMD utilities)
- `message` module (serialized state format)
- `record` module (snapshot ordering consumer)

## Cyber RT Module Mapping
**base** — Lock-free data structures for concurrent individual state access

base 模块的对齐和原子操作保障了个体状态在 pub/sub 传输中的数据完整性，防止 GLB 渲染中出现状态撕裂。

## Dependencies
- `world::base` 模块
- pub/sub 通道基础设施
- 个体状态类型系统（message-safe, standard_layout）

## Sub-tasks
- [ ] 需求分析与接口设计
- [ ] 核心实现（Mixed）
- [ ] 单元测试与边界条件覆盖
- [ ] 集成测试（pub/sub 端到端验证）
- [ ] 文档更新

- [ ] 个体动态输出场景验证通过

base 模块的对齐和原子操作保障了个体状态在 pub/sub 传输中的数据完整性，防止 GLB 渲染中出现状态撕裂。

## Dependencies
- `world::base` 模块
- pub/sub 通道基础设施
- 个体状态类型系统（message-safe, standard_layout）

## Sub-tasks
- [ ] 需求分析与接口设计
- [ ] 核心实现（Mixed）
- [ ] 单元测试与边界条件覆盖
- [ ] 集成测试（pub/sub 端到端验证）
- [ ] 文档更新

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 功能符合 world:: 命名空间规范
- [ ] 与 pub/sub-loop 个体状态处理管道集成正确
- [ ] 通过 ASAN/TSAN 内存安全检查
- [ ] 性能不低于基线（profiler 基准验证）
- [ ] 个体动态输出场景验证通过

世界模型 I/O 层——高吞吐个体状态数据的输入/输出通道，包括批量 TopK 选择、流式编解码、管道并行处理。本需求服务于世界模型的功能需求目标，确保个体（individual）在世界中持续更新数据和位置时，相关功能正确、高效、可靠。

### 个体输出动态关联
io 模块的处理效率直接决定大规模世界（100K+ 个体）的状态更新频率，影响 needle-tools GLB 场景的实时刷新率。

## Dependencies
- `world::io` 模块
- pub/sub 通道基础设施
- 个体状态类型系统（message-safe, standard_layout）

## Sub-tasks
- [ ] 需求分析与接口设计
- [ ] 核心实现（Mixed）
- [ ] 单元测试与边界条件覆盖
- [ ] 集成测试（pub/sub 端到端验证）
- [ ] 文档更新

- [ ] 个体动态输出场景验证通过

`world::io` 模块缺陷修复：: NaN implementation is different on host and device

### pub/sub-loop 上下文
世界模型 I/O 层——高吞吐个体状态数据的输入/输出通道，包括批量 TopK 选择、流式编解码、管道并行处理。本需求服务于世界模型的缺陷修复目标，确保个体（individual）在世界中持续更新数据和位置时，相关功能正确、高效、可靠。

### 个体输出动态关联
io 模块的处理效率直接决定大规模世界（100K+ 个体）的状态更新频率，影响 needle-tools GLB 场景的实时刷新率。

## Dependencies
- `world::io` 模块
- pub/sub 通道基础设施
- 个体状态类型系统（message-safe, standard_layout）

### 世界模型定位
`world::base` 模块功能需求：: Add support for C++26's `atomic::store_add`

### pub/sub-loop 上下文
世界模型基础设施——内存对齐、原子操作、线程池、信号机制等底层原语，为个体状态的零拷贝传输提供基础保障。本需求服务于世界模型的功能需求目标，确保个体（individual）在世界中持续更新数据和位置时，相关功能正确、高效、可靠。

### 个体输出动态关联
base 模块的对齐和原子操作保障了个体状态在 pub/sub 传输中的数据完整性，防止 GLB 渲染中出现状态撕裂。

## Dependencies
- `world::base` 模块
- pub/sub 通道基础设施
- 个体状态类型系统（message-safe, standard_layout）

## Sub-tasks
- [ ] 需求分析与接口设计
- [ ] 核心实现（Mixed）
- [ ] 单元测试与边界条件覆盖
- [ ] 集成测试（pub/sub 端到端验证）
- [ ] 文档更新

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 功能符合 world:: 命名空间规范
- [ ] 与 pub/sub-loop 个体状态处理管道集成正确
- [ ] 通过 ASAN/TSAN 内存安全检查
- [ ] 性能不低于基线（profiler 基准验证）
- [ ] 个体动态输出场景验证通过
